prevalence.source: Indian rheumatology cohort syntheses + regional epidemiology reports

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to rheumatology/nephrology
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis supported by ACR/EULAR criteria, ANA and immunologic markers; renal biopsy for nephritis when available

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; constrained by specialist access and monitoring costs

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ANA testing increasingly available; specialized panels and renal biopsy concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Hydroxychloroquine, steroids, immunosuppressants for organ involvement; biologics in select cases; pregnancy risk management in specialist services
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Specialist shortage, out-of-pocket lab monitoring costs, and infection risk with immunosuppression
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic HCQ and expanding tertiary rheumatology services
